originalTitle: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
title: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
content: Saadia Mosbah campaigned for migrants, particularly after President Kais Saied said they posed a demographic threat to the country.

originalTitle: Desperate for food, drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
title: A Sweet Tree Helps Our Friends in Africa!
content:
Hi there, junior news explorers! I'm your friend, Reporter Roar, bringing you exciting stories from across our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we have a very special story about a big challenge and an even bigger helper. Imagine waking up and feeling very, very hungry. And imagine that there isn't much food around because it hasn't rained for a very, very long time. This is happening to some of our friends in countries like Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia. But guess what? A truly amazing tree is stepping up to help! It's called the Gingerbread Tree, and it's making a big difference for many families who need food.

1. When the Rain Goes Away: What is a Drought?

Sometimes, the sun shines so brightly every day, but no rain clouds come. Day after day, week after week, even month after month! This is what we call a 'drought'. When there's a drought, the ground gets very dry and dusty. It's like a giant sandpit! Little plants can't grow, and big plants start to get sad and withered. Animals also find it hard to find grass or water to drink. And when plants don't grow, people don't have fruits, vegetables, or food for their farm animals. This can make many people feel very, very hungry. Did you know? A drought means there has been much less rain than usual for a very long time! It makes rivers shrink and water holes disappear.

2. Meet Our Hero: The Gingerbread Tree!

But don't worry, there's a brave helper in this story! It's a special tree called the Gingerbread Tree. Isn't that a fun name? This tree grows in the wild, which means people don't plant it in farms. It just grows all by itself, often in dry places where other plants struggle. Why is it called the Gingerbread Tree? Because its fruit often smells a little bit like gingerbread cookies! Imagine that – a tree that smells like a yummy treat! Did you know? The Gingerbread Tree is also called 'Mkoma' in some places, and its scientific name is *Hyphaene compressa*. That's a big fancy name for our helpful tree!

3. What Does the Gingerbread Tree Give Us?

The most special part of the Gingerbread Tree is its fruit. These fruits are round or oval, and they grow on the branches. When the fruit is ripe, people can pick it. The outside is usually smooth and hard, and inside there's a part that you can eat. It might not taste exactly like a gingerbread cookie, but it's sweet and nourishing. It gives people energy and helps them not feel so hungry. People can eat the fruit fresh, right after picking it from the tree. It's a quick and easy snack from nature! Did you know? Sometimes, people even grind the fruit into a type of flour. They can then use this flour to make porridge or other simple foods, which helps feed many people!

4. How is the Tree Helping Our Friends?

Right now, in places like Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia, many families are having a very hard time finding food because of the long drought. When their usual crops, like maize or beans, can't grow, the Gingerbread Tree becomes super important. It's like a secret pantry in the forest! People go out into the wild to find these special trees. They carefully gather the fruits, bringing them home to their families. This wild food helps them get through tough times when there isn't enough other food. It's a gift from nature, helping keep tummies full. Did you know? Many plants and trees in Africa have been helping people survive for hundreds of years, especially when farm food is scarce. Nature is full of clever solutions!

5. Helpers From All Over the World

It's not just the Gingerbread Tree helping. Many kind grown-ups and groups are also working hard to help our friends. There's a special group called Oxfam. They are like a big team of helpers who work all over the world, especially when people are in trouble. Oxfam and other groups are bringing food, clean water, and medicine to families who need it most. They want to make sure everyone is safe and healthy. They are helping about 26 million people in Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia. That's a super big number, almost like everyone in lots of big cities put together! Did you know? 'Humanitarian' means helping other people, especially when they are suffering. So, humanitarian groups are 'helper groups'!

6. Why is the Weather Changing?

Sometimes, the weather changes a lot. It can get much hotter than before, or it can rain much less than it used to. This is something called 'climate change'. It means that the patterns of our weather are shifting. This can make droughts happen more often or last for a longer time. Scientists and smart grown-ups are working to understand why this is happening and how we can help the Earth stay healthy. We can all help a little bit by not wasting water, planting more trees, and taking care of our planet. Did you know? Trees are super important! They help clean the air we breathe and can even help make it rain by putting water into the air!

7. Hope for the Future

Even though it's a tough time for some of our friends, there is always hope! People are very strong and clever, just like the Gingerbread Tree. They are finding ways to help each other and keep going. With help from groups like Oxfam, and the amazing gifts of nature like the Gingerbread Tree, things can get better. We can all learn from this story: be kind to nature, share with our friends, and always look for ways to help. And remember, every little bit of kindness, like a delicious fruit from a special tree, can make a huge difference in someone's day!
summary: In Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia, a special tree called the Gingerbread Tree is helping many people find food during a very long dry period when other food is scarce, with help from groups like Oxfam.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows how nature can help us in tough times, and how people and special helper groups work together to make sure everyone has enough food and care.

originalTitle: UK agrees deal to ease migrant returns to Nigeria
title: Wow! Nigeria and UK Become Even Better Friends!
content:
Hello, wonderful kids of Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here. I have some super exciting news for you today! It's all about two big countries: our very own Nigeria and a country called the United Kingdom, or UK for short. They are making their friendship even stronger, and it's something special for all of us!

A Special Visitor from Nigeria!

Imagine someone very important from your family going on a big trip. Well, our amazing leader, the President of Nigeria, Mr. Bola Ahmed Tinubu, just went on a very important trip! He travelled all the way to the UK. This was not just any trip; it was a "state visit." That means he was invited as a very special guest by the UK government, like when you invite your best friend to your birthday party!

Did You Know?

A state visit is when a leader of one country visits another country to meet their leader. It's a very big deal because they talk about important things and make plans to help each other. It shows how much the two countries trust each other. Our President was welcomed with big smiles and friendly handshakes!

Making a Special Promise

While President Tinubu was in the UK, something wonderful happened. Nigeria and the UK made a special agreement, like a promise or a special "deal." This promise is all about helping people. You see, sometimes people from Nigeria might travel to the UK for different reasons, like for school, work, or to visit family.

Helping Hands for Returning Home

This new promise is like a big helping hand. It's about making it easier and safer for Nigerians who are in the UK to come back home to Nigeria if they want to or need to. It means that if someone needs to return, both countries will work together to make sure they get home safely and soundly. It’s like when you go to play at a friend's house, and your parents make sure you have a safe way to come back home when it's time.

Why Do People Travel Far Away?

Have you ever wondered why people travel to other countries? Sometimes, people move to find new jobs, learn new things in big schools, or even to have new adventures! It's a big wide world, and people explore it for many reasons. But no matter how far they go, home is always a special place. This agreement helps make sure that returning home is a good experience.

Nigeria, A Strong and Kind Country

This deal also shows how strong and kind Nigeria is. Our country cares about all its people, no matter where they are in the world. When countries make agreements like this, it shows that they respect each other and want to work together. It's a sign of a great friendship between nations.

Did You Know?

Nigeria is the most populated country in all of Africa! That means many, many wonderful people live here. We have so many different cultures, languages, and delicious foods. It's a country full of life and joy! Our leaders work hard to make sure everyone is safe and happy.

A Big Moment for West Africa!

Now, here's another super exciting part of this news! President Tinubu's visit was extra special because he is the first leader from West Africa to visit the UK like this in a very, very long time. Can you guess how long? Ready? It has been 37 years! That's even older than your parents or grandparents!

What is West Africa?

Africa is a huge continent, right? West Africa is just a part of it, like how your town is a part of your state. Countries like Ghana, Senegal, and our own Nigeria are all in West Africa. So, for a West African leader to have such an important visit means that our part of Africa is being recognized and celebrated on the world stage!

Friendship Across the Seas

This visit and the new promise mean that Nigeria and the UK are building an even stronger friendship. They will talk more, help each other more, and work together on important things. When countries are friends, it makes the whole world a better and safer place for everyone. It shows that even if people live very far apart, they can still be good neighbours and partners.

Did You Know?

The United Kingdom is an island country, meaning it's surrounded by water! It's very far from Nigeria, across a big ocean. But even with so much distance, our leaders can still meet and make good plans together. Technology like aeroplanes helps them travel quickly to meet face-to-face.

Why This is Good News for Us All

So, why is this important for you, our bright young future leaders? Because it means that our country, Nigeria, is making important connections and showing how strong and friendly it is. It means that our leaders are working hard to make life better and safer for everyone. When our country is strong, we all benefit!

Let's Celebrate Friendship!

This is a wonderful story of friendship and helping hands between countries. It teaches us that being kind and working together can achieve great things. Let's all celebrate this big news for Nigeria and for all of West Africa! Hooray for friendship and cooperation!
summary: Nigeria's President visited the UK and made a special promise to help Nigerians in the UK come home safely, showing strong friendship and making history for West Africa.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows Nigeria is a strong country that cares for its people everywhere, and it builds friendship with other nations, making the world a safer and happier place for everyone.
